title: Continuous Bag of Words (CBOW)
concept: CBOW predicts a target word based on the surrounding context words, best for frequent words and small vocabularies.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym CBOW stand for?
exercises.explain: CBOW stands for Continuous Bag of Words, a popular model architecture used to learn word representations.

cards.title: Fill in the Blanks!
cards.body: Have you ever played a game where someone blanks out a word in a sentence, and you have to guess what it is? Your brain uses the surrounding words as clues. Computers do the exact same thing using a method called CBOW!

cards.title: What is CBOW?
cards.body: Continuous Bag of Words (CBOW) is a smart way to teach computers language. Instead of reading blindly, CBOW looks at a group of surrounding words (the context) and tries to predict the single word that fits right in the middle (the target).

cards.title: The Missing Puzzle Piece
cards.body: Imagine you are trying to guess a missing word in a sentence by looking at the words before and after it. If you see 'The ___ barked loudly', you use the context words 'The' and 'barked' to guess the target word: 'dog'. CBOW does exactly this!

cards.title: Predicting 'sat'
cards.body: If our sentence is 'The cat sat on the mat' and our target word is 'sat', CBOW looks at the context words: ['The', 'cat', 'on', 'the', 'mat']. It uses all of these surrounding words together to guess that the missing middle word is 'sat'!

cards.title: Does Word Order Matter?
cards.body: You might think CBOW cares about the order of the context words, but it actually doesn't! It throws them all into a 'bag' and averages them together. That's why it is called a 'Bag of Words'—the exact order of the clues doesn't change the recipe.

cards.title: Inside CBOW
cards.body: Let's compare the inputs and outputs of the CBOW model to see how it processes data.

exercises.prompt: When is the CBOW model most effective?

options.text: When predicting rare and unusual words
For frequent words and smaller vocabularies
When we want to ignore the context completely

exercises.explain: CBOW is highly efficient and works best when training on frequent words because it averages context words together, making it faster and great for smaller vocabularies.

exercises.prompt: CBOW predicts a target word based on its surrounding context words.
exercises.explain: This is true! CBOW stands for Continuous Bag of Words, and its main job is to predict a middle target word using the words around it.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how CBOW works.
exercises.sentence: CBOW uses surrounding _____ words to predict a single target word.
exercises.explain: CBOW uses the surrounding 'context' words as clues to predict the single 'target' word in the middle.

exercises.prompt: Two AI students are describing how CBOW processes a sentence. Who is correct?

options.text: AI A: 'CBOW takes one target word and uses it to guess all the surrounding context words.'
AI B: 'CBOW takes multiple surrounding context words and uses them to guess the single target word.'
AI C: 'CBOW randomly shuffles all words in a database without looking at their neighbors.'

exercises.explain: AI B is correct. CBOW predicts a single target word from multiple context words, whereas predicting multiple context words from a single target word is the job of the Skip-gram model.

exercises.prompt: How does the Continuous Bag of Words (CBOW) model make its prediction?

options.text: It ignores the context words and guesses randomly
It combines the surrounding context words to predict the middle target word
It uses one target word to predict many different context words
It only looks at the very first word of the entire book

exercises.explain: CBOW works by taking all the surrounding context words, averaging their vector representations together, and using that combined information to predict the single missing target word in the middle.
